session_note: S105 - Owner quickmatch playtest fixes + new directives. (1) Player-One lag while Player-Two smooth -> host-authoritative cost on the render thread; make it SMOOTH REGARDLESS OF HOST + scalable for future real players. (2) Chewers gnaw silently -> reliable+louder+wired gnaw. (3) Line+4-Spirals no turret -> BY DESIGN (needs 7); fix = codex shows EXACT recipes, viewable when LOCKED. NEW owner directives: codex exact recipes for godlies/towers/connectors; spawns ~20% quicker + FULLY RANDOM (today 0xc0ffee fixed seed replays identical every match); 'best most effective scalable' smoothness. Owner: 'the all inclusive broad and targeting PDR is APPROVED ... work to the best of your ability' = unlock_source:user. FULL tier (P3 netcode architecture). PDR: .claude/plans/2026-06-25_PDR_Session_105_Smoothness_Spawn_Codex_Gnaw.md
council_result: S105 design via background workflows: (a) diagnosis+adversarial-verify of the 3 bugs confirmed root causes against code; (b) architecture judge-panel (wf_22b3c4c6) profiling host per-frame cost + scoring 3 approaches (worker-sim / in-frame-budget+optimize / symmetric-lockstep). Supervisor (Opus) verified each root cause by direct code read: laserTurret needs 1 Line+7 Spirals (4 cannot match, by design); gnaw synth+FSM correct (ATTACKING 300 ticks, gate fires 5x/chew on host); spawn 'always same order' = fixed 0xc0ffee seed, not a non-random picker. DETERMINISM guardrail on every priority: spawner is host-only so reseed needs no wire change; replay tests pin their own seeds; render-only SFX; no wall-clock in sim.

priorities.label: S105 P1 - Fully-random + 20%-faster spark spawning. SPAWN_RATE_PER_SECOND 0.15->0.1875 (LOCKED Item 3 amendment, owner-authorized). spawner.reseed(baseSeed) reseeds all 5 streams + countdowns; main.ts draws a fresh random base seed at START_GAME (host-only) so every match differs. No wire change (spawner is host-only; client mirrors via snapshot). Replay-safe (tests pin own seeds; save captures rng words).
priorities.check_method: S105 P1 DONE (de9c799). Gates: tsc 0; vitest 1653/1653 (+3 reseed tests: same-base-seed->identical, diff-seed->different, reseed-moves-off-boot-0xc0ffee); build entry 598.0/750 KiB (152 headroom). ROOT CAUSE was a FIXED boot seed (main.ts SEED=0xc0ffee), not a non-random picker (ALL_SPARK_TYPES has 6 shapes + rngPick already random) -> identical sequence every match. FIX: Spawner.reseed(baseSeed) setStates all 5 streams via SPAWNER_STREAM_OFFSETS (single source of truth, mirrors main.ts boot derivation) + re-samples countdowns; reseedForNewMatch() in main.ts draws Math.random base seed at EVERY host begin (solo/vs-bots/networked-host onBeginMatch wrap) + sets world.rngSeed; vs-bots also seeds BotManager from the same draw. SPAWN_RATE_PER_SECOND 0.15->0.1875 (LOCKED Item 3 owner-authorized amendment). DETERMINISM verified: spawner+sim are host-only (joiner mirrors via NetSnapshot) so no wire/PROTOCOL change; replay tests pin own seeds (unaffected); save captures rng WORDS via getState; rainbow/sudoku RNG derive from world.rngSeed but are host-only + replay-captured. nextId NOT reset on reseed (monotonic, no id collision). NOTE runtime spawn-feel is owner playtest (WebGL rAF).
priorities.objective: Each match a different unpredictable spawn sequence; primitives arrive ~20% sooner. Determinism/replay preserved.
priorities.scope: src/constants.ts (SPAWN_RATE_PER_SECOND), src/game/spawner.ts (reseed), src/main.ts (random base seed at START_GAME), + spawner reseed test.
priorities.testing: tsc 0; vitest green incl. replay/spawner determinism; reseed test (diff seed -> diff sequence, same seed -> identical); build < 750 KiB.

priorities.label: S105 P2 - Codex EXACT recipes + reveal-when-locked (the real fix for the 4-vs-7 spirals turret). main.ts recipeHint() exact for voltkin/pentagram/laserTurret/helga/NONET; codexOverlay shows recipe on LOCKED tiles (name/sprite stay surprise); combos tab adds effect line.
priorities.check_method: S105 P2 DONE (11d2438). Gates: tsc 0; build entry 598.2/750 KiB; codexOverlay stays a LAZY 15.45 KiB chunk (off entry — M12 invariant from S104 preserved). ROOT of the turret 'bug' = pure discoverability: recipe needs 1 Line + 7 Spirals (laserTurret.ts) but the codex hid the recipe behind the unlock gate ('??? / build it to reveal') so you couldn't check it before building. FIX: codexOverlay.makeSpriteTile now renders the recipeHint in BOTH locked+unlocked states (only the NAME + character art stay hidden as the brother-surprise reveal). Made all recipeHints EXACT (main.ts): voltkin '4 Squares then 4 Triangles in one straight chain' (was cryptic 'lightning meets a screen'), turret explicitly '7 spirals, not 4', pentagram/HELGA with totals. Connectors (combos tab) already show their 2-shape glyph recipe (dimmed when undiscovered) — recipe visible, result-name is the reveal, consistent treatment. Render/string only, zero sim/determinism impact. Runtime codex render is owner playtest (WebGL).
priorities.objective: Every godly/tower/connector shows its exact build recipe, checkable BEFORE building.
priorities.scope: src/main.ts (recipeHint), src/render/codexOverlay.ts (locked-tile recipe reveal + combo effect line).
priorities.testing: G+C opens with zero console errors; locked tiles render recipe text; tsc 0; vitest green.

priorities.label: S105 P3 - Smooth regardless of host (scalable). Per architecture judge-panel (wf_22b3c4c6): adopt recommended approach + shippable-now change list; log milestone follow-ups. Bound + cheapen the dominant host per-frame cost; keep render decoupled from sim; worker-authority migration logged as scalable milestone.
priorities.check_method: S105 P3 DONE (dcdfdcb). Gates: tsc 0; vitest 1653/1653 (net/sync 60 + replay 21 green — the determinism-sensitive surface); build entry 598.1/750 KiB. Architecture judge-panel (wf_22b3c4c6, 5 agents, profiled host per-frame cost): DOMINANT host-only cost = the 10Hz full-world snapshot serialize+JSON.stringify (no delta-encode), and it ran INSIDE the physics catch-up while-loop. Panel scored worker-sim(24)/in-frame-budget(29)/lockstep(15); chose IN-FRAME-BUDGET now + WORKER-SIM as the scalable end-state milestone. SHIPPED: Step 1 — moved the host send OUT of the physics drain to ONE post-loop wall-clock ~10Hz send (performance.now gate + >=SNAPSHOT_INTERVAL_TICKS fresh-state floor; removed the in-loop NONET-freeze replica, post-loop send covers it). Step 2 — chewer render trim (skip near-faded draw; body ring 22->16 segs). Both determinism-NEUTRAL (only WHEN we serialize / how the read-only mirror draws — no reducer/RNG/snapshot-content change). DEFERRED to the logged milestone (see carry_forward): worker-sim cutover (host becomes render-only == client; worker boundary == future dedicated-server boundary), snapshot pooling (pairs with delta-encode = Milestone B, the real complexity-class fix), runHostTick extraction, STATEHASH+transcendental determinism instrument, collision 64->8 grid-rebuild (Milestone C, needs new stepPhysics replay test). Honest scope: this is a real felt win for the lagging host + the scalable decision; FULL host-independence is the worker milestone. Runtime smoothness is owner playtest.
priorities.objective: Host stays smooth at TD-entity scale; architecture portable toward more players / dedicated authority.
priorities.scope: per workflow reco — main.ts ticker loop, net/sync.ts + save.ts serialization, the per-frame renderers; determinism contract untouched.
priorities.testing: vitest green incl. replay determinism; build < 750 KiB; boot-smoke; owner playtest for felt smoothness.

priorities.label: S105 P4 - Gnaw SFX reliability. chewerRenderer gnaw fires on each bite reliably (host real ticksInState; client wired); playGnawSFX louder; wire creature.ticksInState/chewProgress into NetSnapshot if absent so client hears it.
priorities.check_method: S105 P4 DONE (c2610ab). Gates: tsc 0; vitest render 73/73 (chewerRenderer 10 + audioManager 63), no test pins the gnaw gate; build entry 598.1/750 KiB. DIAGNOSIS: synth (playGnawSFX) + FSM correct (chewer holds ATTACKING 300 ticks; gate fired ~5x/chew on host); state+ticksInState ARE wired to the client mirror (save.ts:411-412,766 — verified, chewProgress correctly stripped). So neither wire nor synth was broken — the gnaw was just MISSABLE: started 1s into a chew (bucket>=1) at 0.2 gain, lost under music + positional distance attenuation (listener at canvas centre; far ATTACKING chewers ~0.6x). FIX (render-only, determinism-neutral): chewerRenderer gate bucket>=1 -> bucket>=0 (fire the instant a chewer latches on + every bite); playGnawSFX peak 0.2->0.36 (final crunch 0.34->0.5). MAX_GNAW_VOICES=3/frame still caps the swarm. NOTE: if owner playtest STILL shows silence, the residual hypothesis is chewers staying SEEKING near the owner's own base (hop mouth-gape looks like chewing) while the real ATTACKING happens at the far enemy base — that's correct behavior, not a bug; the louder/earlier gnaw maximizes audibility regardless. Runtime audibility is owner playtest.
priorities.objective: A chewing chewer is unmistakably audible on host AND client.
priorities.scope: src/render/chewerRenderer.ts, src/render/audioManager.ts, src/net/protocol.ts + src/state/save.ts (wire creature field if needed).
priorities.testing: tsc 0; vitest green; owner playtest hears gnaw.

carry_forward_priorities: SCALABLE-SMOOTHNESS MILESTONE (next dedicated Full-tier session, runtime-flag-gated ?worker=1): worker-sim cutover — move the authoritative sim into a Web Worker so the host main thread becomes render-only (== client) and runs the SAME snapshot-apply path; main becomes a relay (worker->snapshot->render+netTransport.send; wire->intent->worker.postMessage); transport STAYS on main (WebRTC needs navigator). worker boundary == future dedicated-server boundary (same Intent/Snapshot protocol). Biggest playability risk = host-as-client drag feel (reuse sync.ts dragLock reconcile). Verify TITLE<->PLAYING<->POSTGAME teardown + NONET-freeze replication + Safari module-worker + drag feel before flipping the default.

Milestone B (with/after worker): snapshot POOLING (snapshotInto a persistent buffer on the net path, gated by a pooled==fresh equality test across spawn+despawn) + DELTA-ENCODE (diff vs last-sent, send only changed entities) + binary wire format — the real O(total-world)/100ms complexity-class fix. Pooled buffer is the diff/transfer unit.

Milestone groundwork (additive, low-risk, any session): extract runHostTick(world,deps) from the main.ts !isClient sim block (the worker's entry point; guarded by replay tests) + add a STATEHASH (FNV-1a over determinismJson) + a grep audit asserting no Math.sin/cos/pow/exp feeds WORLD STATE in the sim hot path (render-only transcendentals allowed).

Milestone C (determinism-sensitive, own Council + new stepPhysics-replay test FIRST): resolveCollisions 64->8 grid-rebuild (once per substep, not per inner iteration). Secondary sim cost: throttle the Voltkin every-tick bond rescan (O(creatures x bonds)) — also needs a replay baseline.
Leader crown + enhance existing BOND_COMMIT flair (re-scope, not a parallel burst)
